req.title: Immunization record (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall record an Immunization with administered date not in the future. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an Immunization record's administered date must not be in the future. Five test cases are designed: (1) EP positive — a valid past date in a well-formed payload returns 201; (2) NEG negative — a future date returns 422; (3) BVA boundary — today's date (the inclusive upper boundary) returns 201; (4) DT negative — a missing mandatory field (occurrenceDateTime) returns 422; (5) STC positive — verifying the system-level state transition that a valid clinician submission results in a persisted resource (STC covers valid state transitions, hence positive kind). The previous violation was test[4] having kind=negative with heuristic=STC; STC (State Transition Coverage) is corrected to kind=positive because it validates the allowed transition path (authorized clinician role successfully creates the resource), not a rejection path.

tests.name: test_immunization_valid_past_date_returns_201
preconditions: Target FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a minimal valid Immunization payload with occurrenceDateTime set to yesterday's date
POST payload to /Immunization with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an id field
trace: R-HLT-060: administered date not in the future — valid past date must succeed
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_immunization_valid_past_date_returns_201(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, patient_id):
    yesterday = (date.today() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Immunization",
        "status": "completed",
        "vaccineCode": {"coding": [{"system": "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/cvx", "code": "140"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "occurrenceDateTime": yesterday
    }
    response = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Immunization",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert response.status_code == 201
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Immunization"
    assert "id" in body

tests.name: test_immunization_future_date_returns_422
preconditions: Target FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build an Immunization payload with occurrenceDateTime set to tomorrow's date
POST payload to /Immunization with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body indicates date validation failure
trace: R-HLT-060: error pathway — 422 when date > today
R-HLT-060: administered date must not be in the future
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_immunization_future_date_returns_422(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, patient_id):
    tomorrow = (date.today() + timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Immunization",
        "status": "completed",
        "vaccineCode": {"coding": [{"system": "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/cvx", "code": "140"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "occurrenceDateTime": tomorrow
    }
    response = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Immunization",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert response.status_code == 422
    body = response.json()
    assert body is not None

tests.name: test_immunization_today_date_returns_201
preconditions: Target FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build an Immunization payload with occurrenceDateTime set to today's date (boundary value)
POST payload to /Immunization with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an id field
trace: R-HLT-060: boundary — today is the maximum allowed administered date (inclusive upper bound)
R-HLT-060: administered date not in the future — today must succeed
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date
import requests

def test_immunization_today_date_returns_201(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, patient_id):
    today = date.today().isoformat()
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Immunization",
        "status": "completed",
        "vaccineCode": {"coding": [{"system": "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/cvx", "code": "140"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "occurrenceDateTime": today
    }
    response = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Immunization",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert response.status_code == 201
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Immunization"
    assert "id" in body

tests.name: test_immunization_missing_occurrence_date_returns_422
preconditions: Target FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build an Immunization payload omitting the mandatory occurrenceDateTime field
POST payload to /Immunization with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body indicates missing mandatory element
trace: R-HLT-060: error pathway — missing mandatory elements must be rejected
R-HLT-060: decision table branch — mandatory field absent => 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_immunization_missing_occurrence_date_returns_422(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, patient_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Immunization",
        "status": "completed",
        "vaccineCode": {"coding": [{"system": "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/cvx", "code": "140"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"}
        # occurrenceDateTime intentionally omitted
    }
    response = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Immunization",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert response.status_code == 422
    body = response.json()
    assert body is not None

tests.name: test_immunization_clinician_role_state_transition_creates_resource
preconditions: Target FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
System is in a state where no prior immunization with the same logical identity exists
actions: Build a valid Immunization payload with occurrenceDateTime set to yesterday
POST payload to /Immunization using clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and Location or id in body
Optionally GET the returned resource URL to confirm persisted state
expected: Response body contains an id field confirming resource was persisted
System state transitions from pre-write to post-write with the new Immunization stored
trace: R-HLT-060: precondition — only role:clinician may record immunizations
STC: valid state transition from unauthenticated-write-attempt to authorized-write-success must produce persisted resource
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_immunization_clinician_role_state_transition_creates_resource(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, patient_id):
    yesterday = (date.today() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Immunization",
        "status": "completed",
        "vaccineCode": {"coding": [{"system": "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/cvx", "code": "140"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "occurrenceDateTime": yesterday
    }
    response = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Immunization",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert response.status_code == 201
    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Resource id must be present confirming persisted state transition"
    resource_id = body["id"]
    get_response = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Immunization/{resource_id}",
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert get_response.status_code == 200
    assert get_response.json().get("id") == resource_id
